# Matthew Chapter 17 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_017_page_001.jpg, MAT_017_page_002.jpg

### The Transfiguration

1. Six days later, Jesus took Peter, James, and John (James's brother) and led them up a high mountain where they were alone.

2. There he was transformed before their eyes. His face blazed like the sun, and his clothes became dazzling white.

3. Suddenly Moses and Elijah appeared, talking with him.

4. Peter blurted out, "Lord, it's wonderful that we're here! If you want, I'll set up three shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."

5. While he was still talking, a bright cloud enveloped them, and a voice from the cloud said, "This is my Son, whom I love. I am deeply pleased with him. Listen to him!"

6. The disciples were so terrified they fell facedown on the ground.

7. But Jesus came over and touched them. "Get up," he said. "Don't be afraid."

8. When they looked up, they saw only Jesus.

9. As they came down the mountain, Jesus warned them, "Don't tell anyone what you just saw until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead."

10. The disciples asked, "Why do the teachers of the law say that Elijah has to come first?"

11. Jesus replied, "Elijah is indeed coming and will set everything right.

12. But I tell you, Elijah has already come, and they didn't recognize him. They treated him however they wanted. And the Son of Man will suffer the same way at their hands."

13. Then the disciples realized he was talking about John the Baptist.

### Healing a Boy with a Demon

14. When they reached the crowd, a man came and knelt before Jesus.

15. "Lord, have mercy on my son!" he pleaded. "He has seizures and suffers horribly. He keeps falling into fire or water.

16. I brought him to your disciples, but they couldn't heal him."

17. Jesus replied, "What a faithless and corrupt generation! How long must I be with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy to me."

18. Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of the boy. He was healed from that moment.

19. Later, the disciples came to Jesus privately and asked, "Why couldn't we drive it out?"

20. "Because of your little faith," he answered. "I tell you the truth, if you had faith even as small as a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it would move. Nothing would be impossible for you."

21. [This verse does not appear in the earliest manuscripts.]

### Jesus Again Predicts His Death

22. While they were together in Galilee, Jesus told them, "The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into human hands.

23. They will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised to life." The disciples were overwhelmed with grief.

### The Temple Tax

24. When they arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the temple tax came to Peter and asked, "Doesn't your teacher pay the temple tax?"

25. "Of course he does," Peter replied. When Peter went into the house, Jesus spoke first. "What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes and duties — from their own children or from others?"

26. "From others," Peter answered. "Then the children are exempt," Jesus said.

27. "But so we don't cause unnecessary offense, go down to the lake and throw in a line. Open the mouth of the first fish you catch, and you'll find a four-drachma coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us."

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verses 1-8 — The Transfiguration:** This is the most visually stunning scene in the Gospels. For a moment, the veil is pulled back and the disciples see Jesus as he truly is — radiant with divine glory. Moses (the Law) and Elijah (the Prophets) appear, confirming that Jesus is the fulfillment of everything Scripture has pointed to. The divine voice repeats the baptism declaration (3:17) with one critical addition: "Listen to him." Not to Moses, not to Elijah — to him.

**Verse 4 — Peter's suggestion:** Peter wants to build three shelters — putting Jesus on equal footing with Moses and Elijah. The cloud and the voice immediately correct this: Jesus is not one of three. He is the Son. Moses and Elijah are witnesses; Jesus is the subject.

**Verse 20 — "as small as a mustard seed":** The point is not the quantity of faith but its quality. A mustard seed is nearly invisible — but alive. Dead faith the size of a mountain accomplishes nothing. Living faith the size of a seed moves mountains.

**Verses 24-27 — The temple tax:** This charming, almost humorous episode makes a profound point. Jesus, as God's Son, is exempt from the temple tax (just as a king's children don't pay the king's taxes). But he pays it anyway — not out of obligation but to avoid causing offense. Freedom doesn't always mean exercising your rights.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
